lines.line: <!-- This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
- License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
- file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. -->

<meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'none'; object-src 'none'; script-src resource: chrome:; img-src data: blob: https://firefoxusercontent.com https://profile.accounts.firefox.com;">
<!-- This container is to work around bug 1569292 -->
<!-- Default text inputs to readonly to reduce jumping of the field
size on page load since it always starts readonly. -->

<button role="menuitem" class="menuitem-button menuitem-import-browser ghost-button" hidden data-supported-platforms="Win32,MacIntel" data-event-name="AboutLoginsImportFromBrowser" data-l10n-id="about-logins-menu-menuitem-import-from-another-browser"></button>

lines.line: /* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
* License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
* file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
/* Only overwrite the deemphasized text color in non-dark mode. */
text-align: match-parent !important; /* override `all: unset` in the rule below */
/* We can't use `margin-inline-start` here because we force
* the input to have dir="ltr", so we set the margin manually
margin-inline-start: 0; /* Align the button on the start side */
top: 40px; /* This allows the arrow to stay in the correct position, even if the text length is changed */
margin-inline-start: 0; /* Reset button's margin so it doesn't affect the overall grid's width */
position: relative; /* Allows for the hint message to be positioned correctly */
min-width: 0; /* Allow long passwords to collapse down to flex item width */
margin-bottom: 0; /* Align button at the bottom of the row */
font-family: monospace !important; /* override `all: unset` in the rule above */
/* !important is needed to override common.css styling for checkboxes */
margin-block-start: 4px; /* Focus did not display entirely on edit and remove with margin:0 */

export default class LoginItem extends HTMLElement {
* The number of milliseconds to display the "Copied" success message

// Creates anchor element with origin URL
// We use .value instead of .defaultValue since the latter updates the
// content attribute making the password easily viewable with Inspect
// Element even when Primary Password is enabled. This is only run when
// the password is non-empty since setting the field to an empty value
// would mark the field as 'dirty' for form validation and thus trigger
// the error styling since the password field is 'required'.
// This element is only in the document while unmasked or editing.
// In masked non-edit mode we use a different "display" element to render
// the masked password so that one cannot simply remove/change
// The below handleKeydown will be cleaned up when Bug 1848785 lands.
// This is a temporary fix until Bug 1750072 lands
// This is a temporary fix until Bug 1750072 lands
// Add https:// prefix if one was not provided.
// This is a temporary fix until Bug 1750072 lands
// This is a temporary fix until Bug 1750072 lands
// This is a temporary fix until Bug 1750072 lands
// We prompt for the primary password when entering edit mode already.
Removed the functionality to disable copy button on click since it lead to a focus shift to "Sign in to Sync" every time
it was activated by keyboard navigation. This did not happen if the buttons were not disabled. This was done to avoid any
accessibility concerns. These handleEvents for login-command-button will be extracted out in the follow up bug -> Bug 1844869.
// If there is no username, this must be triggered by the password button,
// don't enable otherCopyButton (username copy button) in this case.
// If there is no username, this must be triggered by the password button,
// don't enable otherCopyButton (username copy button) in this case.
// Prevent page navigation form submit behavior.
// No AutoScroll when middle clicking on origin input.
* Helper to show the "Discard changes" confirmation dialog and delay the
* @param {object} event The event to be delayed.
* @param {object} login The login to be shown on confirmation.
* @param {string} type The type of confirmation dialog to display.
* @param {boolean} onConfirm Optional, the function to execute when the confirm button is clicked.
// If the password input (which uses HTML form validation) wasn't connected,
// append it to the form so it gets included in the reset, specifically for
// .value and the dirty state for validation.
* @param {login} login The login that should be displayed. The login object is

* @param {boolean} skipFocusChange Optional, if present and set to true, the Edit button of the
*                                  login will not get focus automatically. This is used to prevent

* Updates the view if the login argument matches the login currently
* @param {login} login The login that was added to storage. The login object is
*                      a plain JS object representation of nsILoginInfo/nsILoginMetaInfo.
* Updates the view if the login argument matches the login currently
* @param {login} login The login that was modified in storage. The login object is
*                      a plain JS object representation of nsILoginInfo/nsILoginMetaInfo.
* Clears the displayed login if the argument matches the currently
* @param {login} login The login that was removed from storage. The login object is
*                      a plain JS object representation of nsILoginInfo/nsILoginMetaInfo.
* Checks that the edit/new-login form has valid values present for their

// Check validity on all required fields so each field will get :invalid styling

* Toggles the login-item view from editing to non-editing mode.
* @param {boolean} force When true puts the form in 'edit' mode, otherwise
*                        puts the form in read-only mode.
// Reset cursor to the start of the input for long text names.

// Swap which <input> is in the document depending on whether we need the

"should not be a selected guid by default"
"Should initially be in no logins view"
"login-list should initially be in no logins view"
"the blank login list item should be hidden initially"
"Create button should not be disabled initially"
"the blank login list item should be visible after clicking on the create button"
"Create button should be disabled after being clicked"
"cancel button should be visible in create mode with no logins saved"
"there should be no placeholder id on the username input in edit mode"
"there should be no placeholder on the username input in edit mode"
info("waiting for login to get added to storage");
"Should no longer be in no logins view"
"login-list should no longer be in no logins view"
"the blank login list item should be hidden after adding new login"
"Create button shouldn't be disabled after exiting create login view"
"Stored login should only include the origin of the URL provided during creation"
"Stored login should have username provided during creation"
"Stored login should have password provided during creation"
"there should be a placeholder on the username input when not in edit mode"
info("waiting for login to get modified in storage");
}, "waiting for the login to get updated");
"Stored login should only include the origin of the URL provided during creation"
"login list should have a login per testcase"
"there should be a selected guid before create mode"
"the blank login list item should be hidden before create mode"
"there should be no selected guid when in create mode"
"the blank login list item should be visible in create mode"
"there should be a selected guid after canceling create mode"
"the blank login list item should be hidden after canceling create mode"
"cancel button should be visible in create mode with one login showing"
"login-filter should not be cleared if there was a login in the list"
"the same login should still be visible"
"cancel button should be visible in create mode with no logins showing"
"login-filter should be cleared if there were no logins in the list"
"all logins should be visible with blank filter"
"the first item in the list should be selected"
"waiting until the error message is visible"
"Error message has GUID of existing duplicated login set on it"
"the discard-changes dialog should be hidden before clicking the error-message-text"
"waiting until the existing duplicated login is selected"
"the duplicated login should be selected in the list"

lines.line: # This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
# file, You can obtain one at http://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
# NOTE: New strings should use the about-logins- prefix.
fxaccounts-sign-in-text = Get your passwords on your other devices
## The ⋯ menu that is in the top corner of the page
# This menuitem is only visible on Windows and macOS
login-list-intro-description = When you save a password in { -brand-product-name }, it will show up here.
about-logins-login-list-empty-search-description = There are no results matching your search.
about-logins-login-intro-heading-logged-out2 = Looking for your saved logins? Turn on sync or import them.
login-intro-description = If you saved your logins to { -brand-product-name } on a different device, here’s how to get them here:
login-intro-instructions-fxa2 = Create or sign in to your account on the device where your logins are saved.
login-intro-instructions-fxa-settings = Go to Settings > Sync > Turn on syncing… Select the Logins and passwords checkbox.
about-logins-intro-browser-only-import = If your logins are saved in another browser, you can import them into { -brand-product-name }
about-logins-intro-import2 = If your logins are saved outside of { -brand-product-name }, you can import them from another browser or from a file
login-item-tooltip-message = Make sure this matches the exact address of the website where you log in.
## The date is displayed in a timeline showing the password evolution.
## A label is displayed under the date to describe the type of change.
## The macOS strings are preceded by the operating system with "Firefox is trying to "
## and includes subtitle of "Enter password for the user "xxx" to allow this." These
## notes are only valid for English. Please test in your respected locale.
# This message can be seen when attempting to edit a login in about:logins on Windows.
about-logins-edit-login-os-auth-dialog-message-win = To edit your login, enter your Windows login credentials. This helps protect the security of your accounts.
# This message can be seen when attempting to edit a login in about:logins
# On MacOS, only provide the reason that account verification is needed. Do not put a complete sentence here.
# This message can be seen when attempting to reveal a password in about:logins on Windows.
about-logins-reveal-password-os-auth-dialog-message-win = To view your password, enter your Windows login credentials. This helps protect the security of your accounts.
# This message can be seen when attempting to reveal a password in about:logins
# On MacOS, only provide the reason that account verification is needed. Do not put a complete sentence here.
# This message can be seen when attempting to copy a password in about:logins on Windows.
about-logins-copy-password-os-auth-dialog-message-win = To copy your password, enter your Windows login credentials. This helps protect the security of your accounts.
# This message can be seen when attempting to copy a password in about:logins
# On MacOS, only provide the reason that account verification is needed. Do not put a complete sentence here.
# This message can be seen when attempting to export a password in about:logins on Windows.
about-logins-export-password-os-auth-dialog-message-win = To export your logins, enter your Windows login credentials. This helps protect the security of your accounts.
# This message can be seen when attempting to export a password in about:logins
# On MacOS, only provide the reason that account verification is needed. Do not put a complete sentence here.
about-logins-primary-password-notification-message = Please enter your Primary Password to view saved logins & passwords
confirm-delete-dialog-message = This action cannot be undone.
[1] This will remove the login you’ve saved to { -brand-short-name } and any breach alerts that appear here. You won’t be able to undo this action.
*[other] This will remove the logins you’ve saved to { -brand-short-name } and any breach alerts that appear here. You won’t be able to undo this action.
*[other] Remove all { $count } logins from all devices?

[1] This will remove the login you’ve saved to { -brand-short-name } on all devices synced to your account. This will also remove breach alerts that appear here. You won’t be able to undo this action.

*[other] This will remove all logins you’ve saved to { -brand-short-name } on all devices synced to your account. This will also remove breach alerts that appear here. You won’t be able to undo this action.
about-logins-confirm-export-dialog-message = Your passwords will be saved as readable text (e.g., BadP@ssw0rd) so anyone who can open the exported file can view them.
confirm-discard-changes-dialog-message = All unsaved changes will be lost.
breach-alert-text = Passwords were leaked or stolen from this website since you last updated your login details. Change your password to protect your account.
about-logins-breach-alert-date = This breach occurred on { DATETIME($date, day: "numeric", month: "long", year: "numeric") }
#   $hostname (String) - The hostname of the website associated with the login, e.g. "example.com"
about-logins-vulnerable-alert-text2 = This password has been used on another account that was likely in a data breach. Reusing credentials puts all your accounts at risk. Change this password.
#   $hostname (String) - The hostname of the website associated with the login, e.g. "example.com"
# This is an error message that appears when a user attempts to save
# a new login that is identical to an existing saved login.
#   $loginTitle (String) - The title of the website associated with the login.
about-logins-error-message-duplicate-login-with-link = An entry for { $loginTitle } with that username already exists. Go to existing entry?
# This is a generic error message.
about-logins-error-message-default = An error occurred while trying to save this password.
# Title of the file picker dialog
# The default file name shown in the file picker when exporting saved logins.
# A description for the .csv file format that may be shown as the file type
# Title of the file picker dialog
# A description for the .csv file format that may be shown as the file type
# A description for the .tsv file format that may be shown as the file type
# filter by the operating system. TSV is short for 'tab separated values'.
about-logins-import-dialog-error-conflicting-values-title = Multiple Conflicting Values for One Login
about-logins-import-dialog-error-conflicting-values-description = For example: multiple usernames, passwords, URLs, etc. for one login.
about-logins-import-dialog-error-file-format-description = Incorrect or missing column headers. Make sure the file includes columns for username, password and URL.
about-logins-import-dialog-error-file-permission-description = { -brand-short-name } does not have permission to read the file. Try changing the file permissions.
about-logins-import-dialog-error-unable-to-read-description = Make sure you selected a CSV or TSV file.
about-logins-import-report-description = Logins and passwords imported to { -brand-short-name }.
about-logins-import-report-row-description-no-change = Duplicate: Exact match of existing login
##  $field (String) - The name of the field from the CSV file for example url, username or password
